retrieved_kb_context:
## Retrieved KB Context for LLM Planning
Use these similar projects as calibration signals for task scope, FR/NFR classification, complexity levels, and dependency hints. Do not copy tasks unless they are implied by the new requirements.
Hard constraints for this run: emit one task per requirement line, do not merge repeated source lines, keep security/performance/compliance/availability/mobile/localization/privacy constraints as NFRs, and keep titles action-oriented (Implement/Integrate for FR; Enforce/Optimize/Enable for NFR).

### CLINIC project, critic_score=0.92:
  - Implement Patient Registration (FR, C3)
  - Implement Appointment Booking (FR, C3)
  - Implement Doctor Consultation View (FR, C2)
  - Implement Invoice Generation (FR, C3)
  - Implement Email Reminder Service (FR, C3)
  - Implement User Authentication (FR, C2)
  - System Performance NFR - 2s response (NFR, C3)
  - Data Security and Access Control NFR (NFR, C3)

### HOSPITAL project, critic_score=0.93:
  - Implement Patient Triage Queue (FR, C4)
  - Implement Appointment Scheduling (FR, C3)
  - Implement EHR Patient Record Access (FR, C4)
  - Implement Lab Order Workflow (FR, C4)
  - Implement Discharge Summary Generation (FR, C3)
  - Implement Role-Based Clinical Access (FR, C4)
  - HIPAA Audit Trail NFR (NFR, C4)
  - Clinical Data Availability NFR (NFR, C4)

Apply similar FR/NFR classification and complexity levels to the new requirements below, while preserving exact source references and the one-line-to-one-task mapping.

high_complexity_tasks.title: Optimize system uptime and reliability constraints

coverage_by_requirement.original_requirement: The system should Register new patients using national ID and contact details.
The system should Book and manage patient appointments for available doctors.
Actor: Doctor — The Doctor should Allow doctors to view patient history and record diagnosis.
The system should Generate itemized invoices for consultations and lab tests.
The system should Send appointment reminders to patients via email.
The system should maintain high availability.
task_titles: Optimize system uptime and reliability constraints

coverage_by_requirement.original_requirement: The system should be secure and accessible only to authorized users.
The system should support both Arabic and English languages.
The system should respond within two seconds for all standard operations.

stage_summaries.headline: Stage 1 establishes account and identity foundations
titles: Optimize system uptime and reliability constraints

stage_summaries.headline: Stage 2 delivers user communications and alerting

risk_signals: Non-functional scope is substantial, so quality engineering should run in parallel with feature delivery.
2 high-complexity tasks sit on the roadmap and deserve early prototyping.

committee_brief.domain_inference: Likely domain: hospital operations and clinical workflow management.
committee_brief.scope_assessment: Scope includes 6 functional task(s) and 4 non-functional task(s); 0 task(s) sit outside the confirmed baseline because they are optional or low-confidence.
assumption_log: RBAC and permission boundaries were inferred as a structural control because multiple user roles appear in the source requirements.
Dependency sequencing was inferred for 4 task(s) to produce an executable implementation order beyond the raw prose order.
committee_brief.confidence_signal: Planning confidence is high: no low-confidence tasks were detected in the current plan.
committee_brief.graph_summary: 9 requirements -> 10 atomic tasks across 3 delivery sprints. Critical delivery path: T001 -> T002 -> T004 (3 hops, ~6 story points, estimated 5 weeks with a 2-person team). Coordination hotspot: Implement Patient Registration blocks 3 downstream tasks and should be closed by Sprint 1. Parallel execution opportunity: 6 tasks can start immediately in Sprint 1. Effort breakdown: 250 estimated hours | FR: 120h | NFR: 130h.
